notes.content: The Computational Biology major is designed for highly motivated students interested in interdisciplinary studies in life sciences, behavioral sciences, and engineering and computer sciences. The major provides foundations in mathematical modeling, information processing, and control and system analysis, with an emphasis on quantitative ideas and methodologies. Mathematical and other analytical skills are essential in the major. To be considered for this major, you must complete the following by the end of spring before transfer: REQUIRED : One year of General Chemistry with Lab and One and half years of Calculus through Multivariable. Two or more additional courses from: One course in Linear Algebra, One course in Differential Equations, One and a half years of Calculus-based Physics (Mechanics, Electricity, Magnetism, etc), One year of Biology with Lab for the major, One Intro course in Computer Science or Computer Programming, and One Statistics course (equivalent to UCLA's Stats 10 or Life Science 40). All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://cs.ucla.edu/C&SB, https://catalog.registrar.ucla.edu/ and https://admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.
notes.contentHtml:
The Computational Biology major is designed for highly motivated students interested in interdisciplinary studies in life sciences, behavioral sciences, and engineering and computer sciences. The major provides foundations in mathematical modeling, information processing, and control and system analysis, with an emphasis on quantitative ideas and methodologies. Mathematical and other analytical skills are essential in the major.


To be considered for this major, you must complete the following by the end of spring before transfer:

  • REQUIRED:
  • One year of General Chemistry with Lab and
  • One and half years of Calculus through Multivariable.
  • Two or more additional courses from:
  • One course in Linear Algebra,
  • One course in Differential Equations,
  • One and a half years of Calculus-based Physics (Mechanics, Electricity, Magnetism, etc),
  • One year of Biology with Lab for the major,
  • One Intro course in Computer Science or Computer Programming, and
  • One Statistics course (equivalent to UCLA's Stats 10 or Life Science 40).

All courses must be taken for a letter grade.


For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://cs.ucla.edu/C&SB, https://catalog.registrar.ucla.edu/ and https://admission.ucla.edu.


PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.

notes.content: The AP Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep: A score of 5 on the AP Calculus AB exam will satisfy MATH 31A. A score of 4 on the AP Calculus BC exam will satisfy MATH 31A. A score of 5 on the AP Calculus BC exam will satisfy MATH 31A and 31B. The IB Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep: A score of 6 or higher on the IB HL Math Analysis & Approaches exam will satisfy MATH 31A.
notes.contentHtml:
The AP Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep:

  • A score of 5 on the AP Calculus AB exam will satisfy MATH 31A.
  • A score of 4 on the AP Calculus BC exam will satisfy MATH 31A.
  • A score of 5 on the AP Calculus BC exam will satisfy MATH 31A and 31B.


The IB Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep:

  • A score of 6 or higher on the IB HL Math Analysis & Approaches exam will satisfy MATH 31A.
